head.title: Disability and the Worship of Work
head.description: Danilyn Rutherford’s “Disability and the Worship of Work” (published in Somatosphere, 2025) explores how the moral emphasis on labor has shaped the treatment of disabled people, from early eugenics to current Medicaid debates in the United States.

title: Disability and the Worship of Work
featuredImageAlt: Protestant disability care institution Alsterdorfer Anstalten in Hamburg in the 1930s
body: Dagmar Herzog’s *The Question of Unworthy Life* challenges the believe that a life needs to be useful to be meaningful. What can history teach us about the predicaments faced by disabled Americans today? See [https://somatosphere.net/disability-and-the-worship-of-work/](https://somatosphere.net/disability-and-the-worship-of-work/)
